outgoing_links.target_title: Fahd bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud

first_paragraph: The **Prix Foy** is a [Group 2](/wiki/Conditions_races "Conditions races") [flat](/wiki/Flat_racing "Flat racing") [horse race](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in France open to [thoroughbred](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") [colts](/wiki/Colt_(horse) "Colt (horse)") and [fillies](/wiki/Filly "Filly") aged four years or older. It is run at [Longchamp](/wiki/Longchamp_Racecourse "Longchamp Racecourse") over a distance of 2,400 metres (about 1½ miles), and it is scheduled to take place each year in September.

The **Prix Foy** is a [Group 2](/wiki/Conditions_races "Conditions races") [flat](/wiki/Flat_racing "Flat racing") [horse race](/wiki/Horse_racing "Horse racing") in France open to [thoroughbred](/wiki/Thoroughbred "Thoroughbred") [colts](/wiki/Colt_(horse) "Colt (horse)") and [fillies](/wiki/Filly "Filly") aged four years or older. It is run at [Longchamp](/wiki/Longchamp_Racecourse "Longchamp Racecourse") over a distance of 2,400 metres (about 1½ miles), and it is scheduled to take place each year in September.

The race serves as a trial for the [Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe](/wiki/Prix_de_l%27Arc_de_Triomphe "Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe"), which is held at the same venue three weeks later.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Prix_Foy&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The event is named in memory of Henri Foy (1872–1954), a long-serving member of the Société d'Encouragement, a former governing body of horse racing in France. It was established in 1955, and originally called the Prix Henri Foy.

The race was initially contested over 2,300 metres, and for a period it was open to horses aged three or older. It was cut to 2,200 metres in 1961, and the minimum age was raised to four in 1967. Its title was shortened to the Prix Foy in 1969.

The present system of race grading was introduced in 1971, and the Prix Foy was given Group 3 status. It was subsequently run on the same day as the [Prix Niel](/wiki/Prix_Niel "Prix Niel"), a similar event restricted to three-year-olds.

The race was extended to 2,400 metres in 1979. It was promoted to Group 2 level in 1998.

Three winners of the Prix Foy have achieved victory in the same year's Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe – [Allez France](/wiki/Allez_France "Allez France") (1974), [Sagace](/wiki/Sagace "Sagace") (1984) and [Waldgeist](/wiki/Waldgeist_(horse) "Waldgeist (horse)") (2019). Three horses have won the Arc after being defeated in this event – [Gold River](/wiki/Gold_River_(horse) "Gold River (horse)") (1981), [All Along](/wiki/All_Along "All Along") (1983) and [Subotica](/wiki/Subotica_(horse) "Subotica (horse)") (1992).

Prix Foy is eligible for geldings from 2020.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Records

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Prix_Foy&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Records")]

**Most successful horse (2 wins):**

* [Allez France](/wiki/Allez_France "Allez France") – *1974, 1975*
* [Sagace](/wiki/Sagace "Sagace") – *1984, 1985*
* [Orfevre](/wiki/Orfevre "Orfevre") – *2012, 2013*
* [Waldgeist](/wiki/Waldgeist_(horse) "Waldgeist (horse)") – *2018, 2019*

**Leading [jockey](/wiki/Jockey "Jockey") (9 wins):**

* [Yves Saint-Martin](/wiki/Yves_Saint-Martin "Yves Saint-Martin") – *Suffren (1960), Acer (1964), Petrone (1968), Snow Castle (1972), Allez France (1974, 1975), Sagace (1984, 1985), Mersey (1986)*

---

**Leading [trainer](/wiki/Horse_trainer "Horse trainer") (11 wins):**

* [André Fabre](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9_Fabre "André Fabre") – *Ordinance (1987), Star Lift(1989), In the Wings (1990), Richard of York (1994), Carnegie (1995), Swain (1996), Shirocco (2006), Manduro (2007), Waldgeist (2018, 2019), Place Du Carrousel (2023)*

**Leading owner (7 wins):**

* [Daniel Wildenstein](/wiki/Daniel_Wildenstein "Daniel Wildenstein") – *Petrone (1968), Allez France (1974, 1975), Sagace (1984, 1985), Mersey (1986), [Star Lift](/wiki/Star_Lift "Star Lift") (1989)*
